question: What does HTML stand for?
correctAnswer: Hyper Text Markup Language

question: Which tag is used to create a hyperlink?
correctAnswer: <a>

question: Which tag is used to display an image?
correctAnswer: <img>

question: Which tag creates the largest heading?
correctAnswer: <h1>

question: Which tag is used to create a paragraph?
correctAnswer: <p>

question: Which attribute specifies the URL of a hyperlink?
correctAnswer: href

question: Which tag creates an unordered list?
correctAnswer: <ul>

question: Which tag creates an ordered list?
correctAnswer: <ol>

question: Which tag is used to define a list item?
correctAnswer: <li>

question: Which tag is used to create a line break?
correctAnswer: <br>

question: Which tag makes text bold?
correctAnswer: Both <b> and <strong>

question: Which tag makes text italic?
correctAnswer: Both <i> and <em>

question: Which HTML attribute is used to define inline styles?
correctAnswer: style

question: What is the correct HTML for inserting a background image?
correctAnswer: <body style='background-image:url(image.jpg)'>

question: Which tag defines the title of the document?
correctAnswer: <title>

question: Which tag is used to create a table?
correctAnswer: <table>

question: Which tag defines a table row?
correctAnswer: <tr>

question: Which tag defines a table header cell?
correctAnswer: <th>

question: Which tag is used to create a form?
correctAnswer: <form>

question: What is the correct HTML element for the largest font size?
correctAnswer: <h1>

question: Which HTML tag is used to define an internal style sheet?
correctAnswer: <style>

question: Which attribute is used to specify the character encoding for an HTML document?
correctAnswer: charset

question: Which input type defines a slider control?
correctAnswer: range

question: Which tag is used to embed a JavaScript file?
correctAnswer: <script>

question: What does the 'alt' attribute in an <img> tag do?
options: Provides alternative text for the image
correctAnswer: Provides alternative text for the image

question: Which tag is used to define navigation links?
correctAnswer: <nav>

question: Which attribute makes an input field required?
correctAnswer: required

question: Which tag is used to define a section of a webpage?
correctAnswer: All of the above

question: What is the purpose of the <meta> tag?
options: To display metadata on the page
To provide metadata about the HTML document
correctAnswer: To provide metadata about the HTML document

question: Which HTML tag is used to define emphasized text?
correctAnswer: <em>

question: What does the 'colspan' attribute do in a table?
options: Spans a cell across multiple rows
Spans a cell across multiple columns
correctAnswer: Spans a cell across multiple columns

question: Which tag is used to define a footer for a document?
correctAnswer: <footer>

question: Which input type is used for email input?
correctAnswer: email

question: What is the use of the <canvas> tag?
correctAnswer: To draw graphics via JavaScript

question: Which tag defines the main content of a page?
correctAnswer: <main>

question: Which attribute specifies a tooltip for an element?
correctAnswer: title

question: Which tag is used to embed a video in HTML5?
correctAnswer: <video>

question: What does the 'action' attribute in a form specify?
options: The URL to send form data to
correctAnswer: The URL to send form data to

question: Which tag is used to group table header content?
correctAnswer: <thead>

question: Which tag is used to define a dropdown list?
correctAnswer: <select>

question: What is the correct purpose of the HTML <track> element?
options: To define text tracks for media elements like <video> and <audio>
To trace the loading speed of the webpage
To draw a line across the webpage
correctAnswer: To define text tracks for media elements like <video> and <audio>

question: What does the 'defer' attribute do in a <script> tag?
options: Executes the script after the page has finished parsing
correctAnswer: Executes the script after the page has finished parsing

question: What is the difference between <article> and <section>?
options: <article> is for self-contained content, <section> groups related content
<article> is a block element, <section> is inline
correctAnswer: <article> is for self-contained content, <section> groups related content

question: What is the purpose of the 'rel' attribute in a <link> tag?
options: Specifies the relationship between the document and the linked resource
correctAnswer: Specifies the relationship between the document and the linked resource

question: What is the purpose of the <datalist> element?
options: Creates a list of data values
Provides a list of predefined options for an <input> element
Defines a dataset for a chart
correctAnswer: Provides a list of predefined options for an <input> element

question: Which attribute is used to specify that an input field should automatically get focus when the page loads?
correctAnswer: autofocus

question: What does ARIA stand for in web development?
correctAnswer: Accessible Rich Internet Applications

question: What is the purpose of the <picture> element in HTML5?
options: To display multiple images at once
To provide multiple image sources for different screen sizes
correctAnswer: To provide multiple image sources for different screen sizes

question: What does the 'srcset' attribute in an <img> tag do?
options: Provides multiple image sources for different screen resolutions
correctAnswer: Provides multiple image sources for different screen resolutions

question: What is the Shadow DOM?
options: A hidden copy of the DOM
An encapsulated DOM tree attached to an element, separate from the main DOM
correctAnswer: An encapsulated DOM tree attached to an element, separate from the main DOM

question: What is the purpose of the <template> tag in HTML?
options: To create HTML templates for emails
To hold client-side content that is not rendered when the page loads
correctAnswer: To hold client-side content that is not rendered when the page loads

question: What does the 'async' attribute do in a <script> tag?
options: Loads and executes the script asynchronously
Runs the script after DOM is ready
correctAnswer: Loads and executes the script asynchronously

question: Which HTML element is used to define a web component's custom template?
correctAnswer: <template>

question: What is the purpose of the 'manifest' attribute in the <html> tag?
options: Links to an application cache manifest file
correctAnswer: Links to an application cache manifest file

question: What does the 'contenteditable' attribute do?
options: Allows the user to edit the content of an element
correctAnswer: Allows the user to edit the content of an element

question: What is the role of <base> tag in HTML?
options: Specifies a base URL for all relative URLs in the document
correctAnswer: Specifies a base URL for all relative URLs in the document

question: Which attribute in <form> tag specifies how form data should be encoded?
correctAnswer: enctype

question: What is the purpose of the 'sandbox' attribute in an <iframe>?
options: Restricts the capabilities of the content in the iframe
correctAnswer: Restricts the capabilities of the content in the iframe

question: What does the <wbr> tag do?
options: Wraps text in a bordered box
correctAnswer: Creates a word break opportunity

question: What is the purpose of the 'loading' attribute in an <img> tag?
options: Controls when the browser should load the image (lazy or eager)
correctAnswer: Controls when the browser should load the image (lazy or eager)

question: What does CSS stand for?
correctAnswer: Cascading Style Sheets

question: Which CSS property is used to change the text color?
correctAnswer: color

question: Which CSS property is used to change the background color?
correctAnswer: background-color

question: How do you add a comment in CSS?
correctAnswer: /* comment */

question: Which property is used to change the font size?
correctAnswer: font-size

question: How do you select all elements with class 'intro'?
correctAnswer: .intro

question: How do you select an element with id 'demo'?
correctAnswer: #demo

question: Which property is used to make text bold?
correctAnswer: font-weight

question: Which property sets the space between lines of text?
correctAnswer: line-height

question: Which property is used to set the font type?
correctAnswer: font-family

question: How do you apply a CSS style to all <p> elements?
Which property controls the text alignment?
correctAnswer: text-align

question: Which property sets the width of an element?
correctAnswer: width

question: Which property sets the margin outside an element?
correctAnswer: margin

question: Which property sets the space inside an element's border?
correctAnswer: padding

question: Which property makes an element invisible but still take up space?
correctAnswer: visibility:hidden

question: Which property removes an element from the document flow?
correctAnswer: display:none

question: Which property adds a border to an element?
correctAnswer: border

question: How do you make text italic in CSS?
correctAnswer: font-style:italic

question: Which CSS property controls the element's transparency?
correctAnswer: opacity

question: Which of the following is NOT a valid CSS position property value?
correctAnswer: floating

question: What does the 'box-model' in CSS consist of?
correctAnswer: Content, Padding, Border, Margin

question: Which property is used to create space between flex items?
correctAnswer: gap

question: What does 'display: flex' do?
options: Enables flexbox layout on a container
correctAnswer: Enables flexbox layout on a container

question: Which property aligns flex items along the main axis?
correctAnswer: justify-content

question: Which property aligns flex items along the cross axis?
correctAnswer: align-items

question: What is the purpose of 'z-index' in CSS?
options: Controls the stacking order of elements
correctAnswer: Controls the stacking order of elements

question: Which CSS unit is relative to the font size of the root element?
correctAnswer: rem

question: What is a CSS pseudo-class?
options: A keyword added to a selector that specifies a special state of the element
correctAnswer: A keyword added to a selector that specifies a special state of the element

question: Which pseudo-class selects an element when the mouse hovers over it?
correctAnswer: :hover

question: Which property is used to add a shadow to an element?
correctAnswer: box-shadow

question: What does 'position: absolute' do?
options: Positions element relative to the browser window
Positions element relative to its nearest positioned ancestor
Positions element in the normal flow
correctAnswer: Positions element relative to its nearest positioned ancestor

question: What does 'position: fixed' do?
options: Fixes element relative to its parent
Fixes element relative to the viewport
correctAnswer: Fixes element relative to the viewport

question: Which property controls how a background image repeats?
correctAnswer: background-repeat

question: Which CSS function is used to apply multiple transforms?
correctAnswer: transform property with multiple values

question: What is the purpose of CSS media queries?
options: To apply styles based on device characteristics like screen size
correctAnswer: To apply styles based on device characteristics like screen size

question: Which property controls the overflow of content in an element?
correctAnswer: overflow

question: What does 'float: left' do?
options: Moves the element to the left side of the screen
Floats the element to the left of its container allowing text to wrap around it
correctAnswer: Floats the element to the left of its container allowing text to wrap around it

question: Which property specifies the type of cursor when hovering over an element?
correctAnswer: cursor

question: Which CSS property adds rounded corners to an element?
correctAnswer: border-radius

question: What is the difference between 'em' and 'rem' units?
options: 'em' is relative to parent font size, 'rem' is relative to root font size
'em' is for font size, 'rem' is for margins
'rem' is relative to parent, 'em' to root
correctAnswer: 'em' is relative to parent font size, 'rem' is relative to root font size

question: What does 'CSS specificity' mean?
options: A rule that determines which CSS style is applied when multiple rules match
correctAnswer: A rule that determines which CSS style is applied when multiple rules match

question: What is the CSS 'calc()' function used for?
options: To perform calculations to determine CSS property values
correctAnswer: To perform calculations to determine CSS property values

question: What is the purpose of 'will-change' property?
options: Hints to the browser about which properties will change to optimize performance
correctAnswer: Hints to the browser about which properties will change to optimize performance

question: What is a CSS custom property (variable)?
options: A property defined with -- prefix that can be reused throughout the document
A property unique to one element
correctAnswer: A property defined with -- prefix that can be reused throughout the document

question: What is the 'clip-path' property used for?
options: To define a visible area of an element by clipping it to a specific shape
correctAnswer: To define a visible area of an element by clipping it to a specific shape

question: What is the difference between 'transition' and 'animation' in CSS?
options: 'transition' requires a trigger and moves between two states, 'animation' can run automatically with keyframes
correctAnswer: 'transition' requires a trigger and moves between two states, 'animation' can run automatically with keyframes

question: What does 'grid-template-areas' do?
options: Defines named grid areas for easier layout
correctAnswer: Defines named grid areas for easier layout

question: What is 'stacking context' in CSS?
options: The order CSS files are loaded
A three-dimensional conceptual space where elements are rendered based on z-index and positioning
correctAnswer: A three-dimensional conceptual space where elements are rendered based on z-index and positioning

question: What is the purpose of 'contain' property in CSS?
options: Allows browser to isolate a subtree from the rest of the page for performance
correctAnswer: Allows browser to isolate a subtree from the rest of the page for performance

question: What does the ':nth-child()' selector do?
options: Selects elements based on their position among siblings
Selects the nth type of element
Selects child elements of nth depth
correctAnswer: Selects elements based on their position among siblings

question: What is 'CSS Grid' and how does it differ from Flexbox?
options: Grid is two-dimensional (rows and columns), Flexbox is one-dimensional (row OR column)
Grid is for images, Flexbox is for text
correctAnswer: Grid is two-dimensional (rows and columns), Flexbox is one-dimensional (row OR column)

question: What does 'backface-visibility' property do?
options: Controls whether the back face of a 3D-transformed element is visible
correctAnswer: Controls whether the back face of a 3D-transformed element is visible

question: What is the 'aspect-ratio' property used for?
options: Maintains a specific width-to-height ratio of an element
correctAnswer: Maintains a specific width-to-height ratio of an element

question: What does 'mix-blend-mode' do?
options: Defines how an element's content should blend with the content behind it
correctAnswer: Defines how an element's content should blend with the content behind it

question: What is the purpose of '@keyframes' in CSS?
options: To specify the intermediate steps in a CSS animation sequence
correctAnswer: To specify the intermediate steps in a CSS animation sequence

question: What does the 'isolation' property do in CSS?

Isolates the element from the DOM
correctAnswer: Creates a new stacking context for an element

question: What is the 'writing-mode' property used for?
options: To control the direction in which text flows (horizontal or vertical)
correctAnswer: To control the direction in which text flows (horizontal or vertical)

question: What does 'scroll-snap-type' do?
options: Snaps scroll position to a defined point
Takes a snapshot of the scroll position
correctAnswer: Snaps scroll position to a defined point

question: What is the 'counter-reset' property in CSS used for?
options: Initializes or resets a named CSS counter for use with 'content' property
correctAnswer: Initializes or resets a named CSS counter for use with 'content' property

question: Which keyword is used to declare a variable in JavaScript?

question: How do you write 'Hello World' in an alert box?
correctAnswer: alert('Hello World')

question: How do you write a comment in JavaScript?
correctAnswer: // comment

question: Which operator is used to assign a value to a variable?
What is the correct way to write an array in JavaScript?
correctAnswer: var colors = ['red', 'green']

question: How do you find the length of a string 'Hello'?
correctAnswer: 'Hello'.length

question: Which method converts a string to uppercase?
correctAnswer: toUpperCase()

question: What does 'typeof' operator do?
options: Returns the type of a variable
correctAnswer: Returns the type of a variable

question: Which method is used to write HTML output in JavaScript?
correctAnswer: document.write()

question: How do you declare a function in JavaScript?
correctAnswer: function myFunc()

question: What is the result of '5 + '5' in JavaScript?
Which built-in method returns the largest of zero or more numbers?
correctAnswer: Math.max()

question: How do you round a number to the nearest integer?
correctAnswer: Math.round()

question: How do you remove the last element from an array?

question: How do you add an element to the end of an array?
correctAnswer: arr.push()

question: Which event fires when a user clicks on an HTML element?

question: What does '===' operator do?
correctAnswer: Compares value and type

question: Which method converts JSON to a JavaScript object?
correctAnswer: JSON.parse()

question: Which method converts a JavaScript object to JSON?
correctAnswer: JSON.stringify()

question: How do you check if a variable is undefined?
correctAnswer: typeof variable === 'undefined'

question: What is a closure in JavaScript?
options: A function that closes the browser
A function that has access to its outer function's scope even after the outer function has returned
A way to close event listeners
correctAnswer: A function that has access to its outer function's scope even after the outer function has returned

question: What does 'hoisting' mean in JavaScript?
options: JavaScript's default behavior of moving declarations to the top of the current scope
Raising the priority of a function
Moving code to the top of a file
correctAnswer: JavaScript's default behavior of moving declarations to the top of the current scope

question: What is the difference between 'let' and 'var'?
correctAnswer: 'let' is block-scoped, 'var' is function-scoped

question: What is an arrow function?
options: A function with an arrow icon
A shorter syntax for writing functions using => notation
A function that points to a variable
A function only used in arrays
correctAnswer: A shorter syntax for writing functions using => notation

question: What is the purpose of 'use strict' in JavaScript?
options: Enforces stricter parsing and error handling in JavaScript code
correctAnswer: Enforces stricter parsing and error handling in JavaScript code

question: What does the spread operator '...' do?
options: Expands an iterable into individual elements
correctAnswer: Expands an iterable into individual elements

question: What is destructuring in JavaScript?
options: A syntax to unpack values from arrays or properties from objects into distinct variables
correctAnswer: A syntax to unpack values from arrays or properties from objects into distinct variables

question: What is a Promise in JavaScript?
options: A guarantee that code will run
An object representing the eventual completion or failure of an asynchronous operation
A data type for future values
A function that always returns true
correctAnswer: An object representing the eventual completion or failure of an asynchronous operation

question: What does 'async/await' do in JavaScript?
options: Simplifies working with Promises, making asynchronous code look synchronous
correctAnswer: Simplifies working with Promises, making asynchronous code look synchronous

question: What is the 'this' keyword in JavaScript?
options: Refers to the current HTML element
Refers to the object that is currently executing the code
Refers to the global window object always
correctAnswer: Refers to the object that is currently executing the code

question: What is event bubbling in JavaScript?
options: When an event on a child element propagates up to parent elements
correctAnswer: When an event on a child element propagates up to parent elements

question: What does 'event.preventDefault()' do?
options: Prevents the default browser action for an event
correctAnswer: Prevents the default browser action for an event

question: What is the difference between null and undefined?
options: 'null' is an intentional absence of value, 'undefined' means a variable has been declared but not assigned
correctAnswer: 'null' is an intentional absence of value, 'undefined' means a variable has been declared but not assigned

question: What is a callback function?
options: A function that calls another script
A function passed as an argument to another function to be executed later
A function that returns a value
correctAnswer: A function passed as an argument to another function to be executed later

question: What does 'Array.map()' do?
options: Creates a new array by mapping keys to values
Creates a new array with the results of calling a function for every element
correctAnswer: Creates a new array with the results of calling a function for every element

question: What does 'Array.filter()' do?
options: Creates a new array with elements that pass a test function
correctAnswer: Creates a new array with elements that pass a test function

question: What does 'Array.reduce()' do?
options: Executes a reducer function on each element resulting in a single output value
correctAnswer: Executes a reducer function on each element resulting in a single output value

question: What is the purpose of 'localStorage' in JavaScript?
options: Stores data in the browser with no expiration time
correctAnswer: Stores data in the browser with no expiration time

question: What is 'NaN' in JavaScript?
options: A number value representing 'Not a Number'
correctAnswer: A number value representing 'Not a Number'

question: What is a template literal in JavaScript?
options: A string defined with backticks that allows embedded expressions using ${}
correctAnswer: A string defined with backticks that allows embedded expressions using ${}

question: What is the event loop in JavaScript?
options: A loop that listens for events
A mechanism that handles asynchronous callbacks by monitoring the call stack and task queue
A for loop inside an event handler
A loop that fires DOM events
correctAnswer: A mechanism that handles asynchronous callbacks by monitoring the call stack and task queue

question: What is prototypal inheritance in JavaScript?
options: A mechanism where objects inherit directly from other objects via the prototype chain
correctAnswer: A mechanism where objects inherit directly from other objects via the prototype chain

question: What is the difference between call(), apply() and bind()?
options: call() and apply() invoke function immediately with different argument passing, bind() returns a new function
correctAnswer: call() and apply() invoke function immediately with different argument passing, bind() returns a new function

question: What is a WeakMap in JavaScript?
options: A Map where keys must be objects and are weakly held — allowing garbage collection
correctAnswer: A Map where keys must be objects and are weakly held — allowing garbage collection

question: What is a Generator function in JavaScript?
options: A function that can pause and resume execution using yield keyword
A function that auto-generates test data
A function that returns multiple values at once
correctAnswer: A function that can pause and resume execution using yield keyword

question: What is the difference between microtasks and macrotasks?
options: Microtasks (Promises) run before macrotasks (setTimeout) in the event loop
correctAnswer: Microtasks (Promises) run before macrotasks (setTimeout) in the event loop

question: What is a Proxy in JavaScript?
options: An object that wraps another object and intercepts fundamental operations like get, set
correctAnswer: An object that wraps another object and intercepts fundamental operations like get, set

question: What is memoization in JavaScript?
options: An optimization technique that caches results of expensive function calls
correctAnswer: An optimization technique that caches results of expensive function calls

question: What is the difference between deep copy and shallow copy?
options: Shallow copy copies only the first level, deep copy copies all nested levels
correctAnswer: Shallow copy copies only the first level, deep copy copies all nested levels

question: What is currying in JavaScript?
options: Transforming a function with multiple arguments into a sequence of functions each taking a single argument
correctAnswer: Transforming a function with multiple arguments into a sequence of functions each taking a single argument

question: What is the Temporal Dead Zone (TDZ)?
options: The period between entering scope and initialization where let/const variables cannot be accessed
A zone where time functions are blocked
correctAnswer: The period between entering scope and initialization where let/const variables cannot be accessed

question: What is Object.freeze() used for?
options: Prevents modification of existing properties and values of an object
Makes object immutable permanently in memory
correctAnswer: Prevents modification of existing properties and values of an object

question: What is the purpose of Symbol in JavaScript?
options: A unique and immutable primitive value often used as object property keys
correctAnswer: A unique and immutable primitive value often used as object property keys

question: What does 'Object.create()' do?
options: Creates a new object with the specified object as prototype
correctAnswer: Creates a new object with the specified object as prototype

question: What is tail call optimization?
options: Optimizing the last line of code
A technique where recursive tail calls are optimized to avoid adding new stack frames
Removing the last element from call stack
correctAnswer: A technique where recursive tail calls are optimized to avoid adding new stack frames

question: What is the purpose of 'Reflect' in JavaScript?
options: Provides methods for interceptable JavaScript operations, similar to Proxy traps
correctAnswer: Provides methods for interceptable JavaScript operations, similar to Proxy traps

question: What is function composition in JavaScript?
options: Combining two or more functions to produce a new function
correctAnswer: Combining two or more functions to produce a new function

question: What is the difference between setTimeout and setInterval?
options: setTimeout runs once after a delay, setInterval runs repeatedly at a specified interval
setTimeout is asynchronous, setInterval is synchronous
correctAnswer: setTimeout runs once after a delay, setInterval runs repeatedly at a specified interval

question: What is an IIFE in JavaScript?
options: Immediately Invoked Function Expression — a function that runs immediately after being defined
correctAnswer: Immediately Invoked Function Expression — a function that runs immediately after being defined

question: What is the purpose of 'Object.defineProperty()'?
options: Defines a new property directly on an object with fine-grained control over its behavior
correctAnswer: Defines a new property directly on an object with fine-grained control over its behavior

question: What is used to pass data to components from outside?
correctAnswer: props

question: What is JSX?
options: A JavaScript extension for XML-like syntax in React
correctAnswer: A JavaScript extension for XML-like syntax in React

question: Which hook is used to manage state in a functional component?
correctAnswer: useState

question: What is a React component?
options: A reusable piece of UI that can be a function or class
correctAnswer: A reusable piece of UI that can be a function or class

question: How do you render a component in React?
correctAnswer: ReactDOM.render()

question: What is the virtual DOM?
options: A lightweight in-memory representation of the real DOM used by React to optimize updates
correctAnswer: A lightweight in-memory representation of the real DOM used by React to optimize updates

question: What is the purpose of 'key' prop in a list?
options: To help React identify which items have changed for efficient re-rendering
correctAnswer: To help React identify which items have changed for efficient re-rendering

question: How do you handle a click event in React?
correctAnswer: onClick={handler}

question: What is the default value of state in React if not initialized?
correctAnswer: undefined

question: What does 'setState' do in React?
options: Triggers a re-render by scheduling a state update
correctAnswer: Triggers a re-render by scheduling a state update

question: What file extension is commonly used for React components?
correctAnswer: .jsx or .js

question: What is a functional component in React?
options: A component written as a JavaScript function
A component that only handles functions
A component using only built-in functions
correctAnswer: A component written as a JavaScript function

question: Which method is used to update state in a class component?
correctAnswer: this.setState()

question: What is a prop in React?
options: A property passed to a component from its parent
correctAnswer: A property passed to a component from its parent

question: How do you conditionally render a component in React?
options: Using if-else or ternary operator in JSX
correctAnswer: Using if-else or ternary operator in JSX

question: What does 'import React from react' do?
options: Imports the React library needed to use JSX
correctAnswer: Imports the React library needed to use JSX

question: What is the correct way to apply a CSS class in JSX?
correctAnswer: className='myClass'

question: What is the top-level component in a React app usually called?
correctAnswer: App

question: How do you embed a JavaScript expression in JSX?
correctAnswer: Using {expression}

question: What does 'props.children' refer to?
options: Child components passed between the opening and closing tags of a component
Sub-components defined in the same file
correctAnswer: Child components passed between the opening and closing tags of a component

question: Which hook is used to perform side effects in a function component?
correctAnswer: useEffect

question: What is the purpose of the dependency array in useEffect?
options: Controls when the effect runs — empty array means run once, listed deps mean run on change
correctAnswer: Controls when the effect runs — empty array means run once, listed deps mean run on change

question: What is useContext used for?
options: To consume a React context value without prop drilling
To share state between siblings only
correctAnswer: To consume a React context value without prop drilling

question: What is React Context?
options: A way to pass data through the component tree without prop drilling
correctAnswer: A way to pass data through the component tree without prop drilling

question: What does useRef do in React?
options: Returns a mutable ref object that persists across renders without causing re-renders
correctAnswer: Returns a mutable ref object that persists across renders without causing re-renders

question: What is a controlled component in React?
options: A component controlled by a parent only
A form element whose value is controlled by React state
A component that cannot be modified
correctAnswer: A form element whose value is controlled by React state

question: What is an uncontrolled component?
options: A form element that manages its own state through the DOM using refs
A component that cannot be tested
correctAnswer: A form element that manages its own state through the DOM using refs

question: What is the purpose of useReducer?
options: To manage complex state logic as an alternative to useState
To reduce the number of renders
correctAnswer: To manage complex state logic as an alternative to useState

question: What is React Router used for?
options: Handling navigation and rendering components based on URL in React apps
correctAnswer: Handling navigation and rendering components based on URL in React apps

question: What is the purpose of useMemo?
options: Memoizes the result of a computation to avoid re-computing on every render
correctAnswer: Memoizes the result of a computation to avoid re-computing on every render

question: What is useCallback used for?
options: To memoize a function so it is not recreated on every render
correctAnswer: To memoize a function so it is not recreated on every render

question: What is prop drilling?
options: A technique to drill into props
Passing props through multiple levels of components that don't need them just to reach a deeply nested component
correctAnswer: Passing props through multiple levels of components that don't need them just to reach a deeply nested component

question: What is the difference between state and props?
options: State is managed internally by the component, props are passed from parent to child
Props are internal, state is external
State is immutable, props are mutable
correctAnswer: State is managed internally by the component, props are passed from parent to child

question: What is React Fragments?
options: A way to group multiple elements without adding extra DOM nodes using <> or <Fragment>
correctAnswer: A way to group multiple elements without adding extra DOM nodes using <> or <Fragment>

question: What is the purpose of React.StrictMode?
options: Highlights potential problems in an application during development
correctAnswer: Highlights potential problems in an application during development

question: What is a higher-order component (HOC)?
options: A function that takes a component and returns a new enhanced component
A component that renders above others
The top-level component in a tree
correctAnswer: A function that takes a component and returns a new enhanced component

question: What does the useLayoutEffect hook do?
options: Same as useEffect but fires synchronously after all DOM mutations
correctAnswer: Same as useEffect but fires synchronously after all DOM mutations

question: What is lazy loading in React?
options: Loading components only when they are needed using React.lazy()
correctAnswer: Loading components only when they are needed using React.lazy()

question: What is Suspense in React?
options: A component that lets you wait for something like lazy-loaded components before rendering
correctAnswer: A component that lets you wait for something like lazy-loaded components before rendering

question: What is reconciliation in React?
options: The process React uses to compare the virtual DOM with the previous version to update only what changed
correctAnswer: The process React uses to compare the virtual DOM with the previous version to update only what changed

question: What does React.memo do?
options: It memorizes the state of a component
It is a higher-order component that prevents unnecessary re-renders if props haven't changed
It creates a global store for application data
It forces a component to re-render synchronously
correctAnswer: It is a higher-order component that prevents unnecessary re-renders if props haven't changed

question: What is the Fiber architecture in React?
options: React's internal reconciliation engine that enables incremental rendering and priority-based updates
correctAnswer: React's internal reconciliation engine that enables incremental rendering and priority-based updates

question: What is the purpose of Error Boundaries in React?
options: To catch rendering errors in child components and display a fallback UI
correctAnswer: To catch rendering errors in child components and display a fallback UI

question: What is the difference between useEffect and useLayoutEffect?
options: useEffect runs asynchronously after render, useLayoutEffect runs synchronously after DOM updates but before paint
correctAnswer: useEffect runs asynchronously after render, useLayoutEffect runs synchronously after DOM updates but before paint

question: What is the purpose of React Portals?
options: To render children into a DOM node outside the parent component's DOM hierarchy
To connect React to a server
correctAnswer: To render children into a DOM node outside the parent component's DOM hierarchy

question: What is code splitting in React?
options: Splitting React code into multiple files
Breaking the app bundle into smaller chunks that are loaded on demand to improve performance
correctAnswer: Breaking the app bundle into smaller chunks that are loaded on demand to improve performance

question: What is the purpose of forwardRef in React?
options: To forward a ref through a component to one of its children
correctAnswer: To forward a ref through a component to one of its children

question: What is concurrent mode in React?
options: A set of features allowing React to interrupt rendering work and prioritize more urgent updates
correctAnswer: A set of features allowing React to interrupt rendering work and prioritize more urgent updates

question: What is the useImperativeHandle hook used for?
options: To customize the instance value exposed when using ref with forwardRef
correctAnswer: To customize the instance value exposed when using ref with forwardRef

question: What is the difference between Redux and Context API?
options: Redux is a standalone library with middleware support and DevTools, Context API is built-in but less powerful for complex state
Context API is better than Redux always
correctAnswer: Redux is a standalone library with middleware support and DevTools, Context API is built-in but less powerful for complex state

question: What is the purpose of the useDebugValue hook?
options: To display a label for custom hooks in React DevTools
correctAnswer: To display a label for custom hooks in React DevTools

question: What is React Server Components?
options: Components that run on the server and never send JavaScript to the client
correctAnswer: Components that run on the server and never send JavaScript to the client

question: What is the purpose of the 'key' prop during reconciliation?
options: Helps React uniquely identify elements to determine which items changed, were added, or removed efficiently
correctAnswer: Helps React uniquely identify elements to determine which items changed, were added, or removed efficiently

question: What is startTransition in React 18?
options: Marks state updates as non-urgent so React can prioritize more critical updates
correctAnswer: Marks state updates as non-urgent so React can prioritize more critical updates

question: What is automatic batching in React 18?
options: React automatically batches multiple state updates into a single re-render for performance
correctAnswer: React automatically batches multiple state updates into a single re-render for performance

question: What is a custom hook in React?
options: A JavaScript function starting with 'use' that can call other hooks and encapsulate reusable logic
A hook only for class components
correctAnswer: A JavaScript function starting with 'use' that can call other hooks and encapsulate reusable logic

question: What is the purpose of getDerivedStateFromProps?
options: A static lifecycle method that updates state based on changes in props before rendering
To get initial state from props
To derive new props from state
correctAnswer: A static lifecycle method that updates state based on changes in props before rendering

question: What is the significance of shouldComponentUpdate?
options: Allows you to control whether a component should re-render by returning true or false
correctAnswer: Allows you to control whether a component should re-render by returning true or false

question: What is hydration in React?
options: The process of attaching event listeners to server-rendered HTML so React can take over on the client
correctAnswer: The process of attaching event listeners to server-rendered HTML so React can take over on the client

question: What is the purpose of React.cloneElement?
options: Creates a new React element using an existing element as a starting point, allowing you to modify its props
Duplicates a component in the DOM
correctAnswer: Creates a new React element using an existing element as a starting point, allowing you to modify its props
